title: Pod sage-attention not installing → SDPA fallback → slower LTX sampling. Fix the runtime sage bootstrap (Blackwell/Ada arch-list).
description:
PERF. The product Pod's sage-attention runtime bootstrap (start.sh: pip install sageattention --target=/workspace/cubric/pylibs onto the volume on first boot) PRODUCES NOTHING on the live Pods tested this session: `.sage_arch` stamp empty, `import sageattention` -> ModuleNotFoundError, and the running ComfyUI cmdline has NO `--use-sage-attention`. So every remote LTX gen ran on PyTorch SDPA (the fallback), not sage. Sage is ~2-3x faster than SDPA for diffusion → this is a real sampling speedup left on the table. Confirmed on BOTH a 5090 (Blackwell sm_120) and a 4090 (Ada sm_89) this session.

SUSPECTED CAUSE (from docs/gotchas.md:185, the Builder image learned this): the runtime `pip install sageattention --no-build-isolation` compiles CUDA kernels, but on a GPU-less or arch-mismatched build it 'silently falls back to SDPA'. The Builder image SOURCE-BUILDS sage with `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST="8.6;8.9;12.0"` (Ada=8.9, Blackwell=12.0) — the product Pod's start.sh bootstrap likely lacks the arch list / build deps, so the compile fails and start.sh swallows it as 'non-fatal'. A bare `pip install sageattention` on the live Pod also produced nothing (user ran it).

ALSO: the user noted past sage issues — UNSURE if Blackwell-only or both. gotchas.md:147-149 documents sage as a 2x SLOWDOWN on WINDOWS (Triton JIT fails on embedded python) — but that is LOCAL/Windows-only; Linux Pods are 'unaffected' per that note. So on the Pod, sage SHOULD help — the problem is it never installs.

TASKS:
1. Reproduce: why does the start.sh sage bootstrap produce an empty pylibs? Check start.sh:74-101 (the bootstrap block) — does it set TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST? Does the compile error get logged or swallowed?
2. Port the Builder's working pattern: SOURCE-BUILD sage with the arch list (8.6;8.9;12.0) so it compiles for Ada+Blackwell. Possibly bake into the image instead of runtime-installing (the runtime ~5-15min compile is also slow + fragile).
3. Verify on a live Pod: `.sage_arch` populated, `import sageattention` OK, ComfyUI cmdline shows `--use-sage-attention`, and sampling is measurably faster.
4. Consider per-arch: if Blackwell sage is problematic, allow SDPA on cu128 but sage on cu124 (or vice versa) — settle from test data.

NEEDS AN IMAGE REBUILD (batch with the v0.9.0 cycle: MPI-139 v0.26.0 + MPI-146 per-card VRAM). BRANCH: RunPod / mpi-ci only. Image build scripts live in c:\AI\Mpi\mpi-ci\cubric-vision-pod\ (start.sh sage block) + cubric-vision-builder\install_nodes.sh (the working source-build reference).
activeSessionTitle: Bake sage per-arch + per-card VRAM into product Pod (v0.10.0 batch)
